zh.title: 亚洲疾病问题
zh.description: 著名的框架效应实验，展示表述方式如何影响决策。
zh.question:
想象美国正准备应对一种罕见的亚洲疾病，预计该疾病将导致600人死亡。现有两种与疾病作斗争的方案可供选择。假定对各方案所产生后果的精确科学估算如下所示：

如果采用A方案，200人将生还。
如果采用B方案，有1/3的机会600人将生还，而有2/3的机会无人将生还。

你会选择哪个方案？
zh.hint: 注意：这是收益框架描述。同样的问题如果用损失框架描述，人们的选择往往截然不同。

en.title: The Asian Disease Problem
en.description: Famous framing effect experiment showing how wording affects decisions.
en.question:
Imagine that the U.S. is preparing for the outbreak of an unusual Asian disease, which is expected to kill 600 people. Two alternative programs to combat the disease have been proposed. Assume that the exact scientific estimates of the consequences of the programs are as follows:

If Program A is adopted, 200 people will be saved.
If Program B is adopted, there is a 1/3 probability that 600 people will be saved, and a 2/3 probability that no people will be saved.

Which program do you choose?
en.hint: Note: This is a gain-framed description. The same problem described in loss-framed terms often produces dramatically different choices.

reference: Kahneman, D., & Tversky, A. (1979). Prospect Theory
